description_long: This object is a long, traditional-style rifle with a wooden stock and a lengthy metallic barrel. The stock is a warm brown color, smoothly contoured and extending into a straight grip, typical of older musket or flintlock designs. The barrel is silver-gray, slender, and appears to be made of metal, running the full length of the rifle and ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. The trigger guard and trigger are visible, with a metallic finish, and there is a basic iron sight near the rear of the barrel. The rifle lacks any modern features such as rails, optics, or detachable magazines, suggesting a classic, possibly historical firearm. The overall construction is streamlined, with minimal external components and a focus on simplicity and durability.
description: A long, classic rifle with a wooden stock and a silver metallic barrel, featuring a simple iron sight and traditional design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the wooden stock and long metallic barrel.
description_view_1: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the narrow profile of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rifle's length, stock, and metallic barrel, similar to view 0 but mirrored.
description_view_3: Front view looking down the barrel, showing the circular muzzle and the slim profile of the rifle.
